channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Non-English-speaking home care patients with terminal cancer may have worse end-of-life outcomes than anglophones
item.description: Allophone home care recipients with terminal cancer faced higher risks of death, emergency visits, and hospital admission within the study period.

item.title: Cannabis drinks become America's new pastime, at least for now
item.description: At Fourth of July barbecues, Americans are increasingly choosing cannabis cans over beer.

item.title: Would hunters accept a Lyme disease vaccine?
item.description: Tick season is here, possibly the worst in a decade.

item.title: In Quebec, young adults were the first to be hit by the pandemic
item.description: During the first two years of COVID-19 in Quebec, young adults were typically infected before older age groups across seven waves.
